title: Ripening Moment
definition: The idea that the night under the Bodhi Tree was the culmination of years of training and practice, rather than the beginning.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The night under the Bodhi Tree represented the final performance after years of dedicated ethical, mental, and physical training for enlightenment.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The night under the Bodhi Tree was the culmination of the Buddha's practice and training, which included ethical conduct, mental discipline, and spiritual insight.
difficulty_levels.expert: The ripening moment refers to the idea that the night under the Bodhi Tree was not the beginning of the Buddha's enlightenment journey but the maturation of years of ethical, mental, and philosophical training and practice.

extra_fields.insight: Enlightenment is the result of a lifelong commitment to training, practice, and self-discipline rather than a single event.
